parent.name: David de la Roche Lord Roche

km.Fermoy, Cork, Munster, Ireland: ហ្វេរម៉ូយ, កូuនtយ ខូរក, អៀរឡង់

zh.Fermoy, Cork, Munster, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 科克費爾莫易
zh.lifeSummary: 當John de La Roche於1350年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克費爾莫易時，他的父親David de la Roche Lord Roche51歲，母親Anna Fleming22歲。他於1374年與Lady Amy MacCarthy在愛爾蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1382年被列為sheriff of cork。他去世於1387年在他的家鄉享年37歲。

zh-hans.Fermoy, Cork, Munster, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 科克費爾莫易
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John de La Roche于1350年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 科克費爾莫易时，他的父亲David de la Roche Lord Roche51岁，母亲Anna Fleming22岁。他于1374年与Lady Amy MacCarthy在爱尔兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1382年被列为sheriff of cork。他去世于1387年在他的家乡享年37岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Roche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, Fionnuala, Kevin Patrick, Liam.
Irish (Munster) and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Roach  . Compare   Roch  .

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a rocky outcrop or crag, from Old French roche ‘rock’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the variant Laroche 2. Compare Deroche .

variants.name: Laroche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Normand, Pierre, Jacques, Laurent, Adrien, Cecile, Germain, Marcel.
French:
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Laroche or La Roche, from Old French roche ‘rock’, with feminine definite article la.

variants.name: Larocque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Henri, Marcel, Normand, Valmore, Yves, Achille, Amie, Donat, Emile.

French: habitational name from La Ro(c)que, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a regional variant of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche , compare Laroche ), with feminine definite article la. Compare Laroque .

Altered form of southern French Roquebrune or of its variant Rocquebrune: habitational name from Ro(c)quebrune, the name of several places in the southern part of France. The surname Roquebrune is rare in France, while the variant Rocquebrune is extinct there. Compare Larock , Lerocque , Rockburn , and Rocque 1.

variants.name: Rochet
meanings: French: diminutive of   Roche  , a topographic name meaning ‘rock’, or a habitational name from (Le) Rochet, the name of several places in various parts of France.
Catalan: of French origin (see above). This surname is also found in the Dominican Republic and Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Rochat
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique, Philippe, Pierre.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French roche ‘rock’ (see Roche 2), or a habitational name from Le Rochat, the name of several places in the eastern part of France.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Roquet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Camille.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche and Roque ), or a habitational name from (Le) Roquet, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Rochez
meanings: Walloon and French: variant of   Roche   2 or   Rocher  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John de La Roche was born in 1350, in Fermoy, County Cork, Ireland, his father, David de la Roche Lord Roche, was 51 and his mother, Anna Fleming, was 22. He married Lady Amy MacCarthy in 1374, in Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. In 1382, at the age of 32, his occupation is listed as sheriff of cork. He died in 1387, in his hometown, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de La Roche est né en 1350, à Fermoy, County Cork, Irlande, son père, David de la Roche Lord Roche, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Anna Fleming, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Lady Amy MacCarthy en 1374, à Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. En 1382, à l’âge de 32 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of cork. Il est décédé en 1387, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de La Roche 1350 in Fermoy, County Cork, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater David de la Roche Lord Roche 51 und seine Mutter Anna Fleming 22. Er hat 1374 in Irland Lady Amy MacCarthy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1382, als er 32 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff of cork aufgeführt. Er starb 1387 im Alter von 37 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de La Roche è nato il nel 1350 nel Fermoy, County Cork, Irlanda, il suo padre, David de la Roche Lord Roche, aveva 51 anni e il suo madre, Anna Fleming, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Lady Amy MacCarthy nel 1374, nel Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1382, all'età di 32 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of cork. Morì nel 1387, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 37 anni.

ja.Fermoy, Cork, Munster, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ コルクフエルモイ
ja.lifeSummary: John de La Rocheが1350年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクフエルモイで生まれた時，父David de la Roche Lord Rocheは51歳，母Anna Flemingは22歳であった。1374年にアイルランドでLady Amy MacCarthyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。1382年，32歳の時，職業はsheriff of corkと記録されている。彼は1387年に故郷で37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de La Roche(이)가 1350년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 퍼모이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David de la Roche Lord Roche(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Fleming(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1374년 아일랜드에서 Lady Amy MacCarthy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1382년, 32세의 나이로 그의 직업은 sheriff of cork이었다. 그는 1387년 그의 고향에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de La Roche nasceu em 1350, em Fermoy, County Cork, Irlanda, seu pai, David de la Roche Lord Roche, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Anna Fleming, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Amy MacCarthy em 1374, em Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1382, com 32 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of cork. Ele faleceu em 1387, em sua cidade natal, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de La Roche родился в 1350 г. в местности фирмои, коунти корк, Ирландия, его отцу по имени David de la Roche Lord Roche было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Anna Fleming, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Amy MacCarthy в 1374 г. в местности Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1382 году, в возрасте 32 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff of cork. Он умер в 1387 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 37 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de La Roche nació en 1350, en Fermoy, County Cork, Irlanda, su papá, David de la Roche Lord Roche, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Anna Fleming, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Lady Amy MacCarthy en 1374, en Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. En 1382, a la edad de 32 años, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of cork. Murió en 1387, en su pueblo, a la edad de 37 años.

th.Fermoy, Cork, Munster, Ireland: เฟอรมอย, คูนตี โกรก, ไอร์แลนด์
